mechanism_narrative: Declining federal funds rates reduce corporate borrowing costs and lower equity discount rates, directly boosting S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Rank IC remains below 0.02 over 3-year rolling windows or forward returns show no statistical significance during rate cuts.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly unemployment rate increase exceeding 0.2 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions that compress consumer spending and corporate earnings, driving negative S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 55% of triggered instances despite the unemployment spike.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in real GDP and retail sales indicates synchronized domestic demand expansion that lifts corporate revenues, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Joint positive growth in GDPC1 and RSAFS fails to predict positive 60d returns with an out-of-sample accuracy exceeding 52%.

mechanism_narrative: A contraction in the index of industrial production during a monetary tightening cycle reflects supply-side constraints that outweigh demand, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next week.
failure_condition: INDPRO declines during tightening periods are followed by positive 5d SPX returns in > 60% of cases.

mechanism_narrative: Rising core inflation combined with stable or rising federal funds rates creates a real rate compression environment that forces equity repricing, resulting in negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The joint signal yields a negative rank correlation with 20d returns, or predictive accuracy falls below 48% over rolling 2-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Steady month-over-month growth in total nonfarm payrolls reflects robust labor market expansion that supports wage growth and consumer confidence, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: PAYEMS growth shows zero or negative correlation with 60d forward returns across all tested rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly housing starts decline exceeding 5% signals a sharp contraction in residential investment and construction activity, dragging down related equity sectors and producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next week.
failure_condition: SPX generates positive 5d returns in > 58% of instances following a >5% monthly housing starts drop.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between industrial production growth and unemployment rate declines indicates supply-side inefficiencies that cap corporate profit margins, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The divergence signal fails to predict negative returns with statistical significance or shows positive predictive accuracy in walk-forward tests.
